acts.title: Balakanda: Birth and Bow
acts.setup: King Dasharatha of Ayodhya has three wives but no heirs. He performs the putra-kameshti yajna and receives divine payasam from the gods. His wives bear four sons, Bharata, Lakshmana, and Shatrughna. Rama grows into the ideal prince and wins Sita’s hand by breaking Shiva’s bow at Janakpur.
acts.lesson: The divine descends into the world to restore dharma when adharma rises.

objectives.title: Witness Rama’s birth and the divine payasam
objectives.cue: Stand before the sacred fire and witness the birth ceremony.

completedLine.speaker: Kausalya
completedLine.text: The gods have blessed us. Rama is born to restore dharma to the world.

objectives.citation: Valmiki Ramayana, Bala Kanda, Sargas 15-20 (birth of the four princes)

objectives.title: Break Shiva’s bow at Janakpur to win Sita
objectives.cue: Stand before the great bow and choose your path to Sita.

completedLine.speaker: Janaka
completedLine.text: No man has lifted this bow since Shiva. You have broken it. Sita is yours.

objectives.citation: Valmiki Ramayana, Bala Kanda, Sarga 67 (Shiva dhanusha bhanga)

objectives.title: Marry Sita at Janakpur
objectives.cue: Speak with Sita and receive her bride.

completedLine.speaker: Sita
completedLine.text: I am Sita, daughter of Earth. Where you walk, I shall walk. Where you dwell, I shall dwell.

objectives.citation: Valmiki Ramayana, Bala Kanda, Sargas 73-76 (the wedding at Janakpur)

objectives.title: Accept Vishwamitra’s request to protect his sacrifice
objectives.cue: The sage Vishwamitra has come to Ayodhya. He seeks warriors to protect his sacred fire from demons.

completedLine.speaker: Vishwamitra
completedLine.text: You are young, but the gods have prepared you. Come, let us walk where adharma festers.

objectives.citation: Valmiki Ramayana, Bala Kanda, Sargas 21-30 (Vishwamitra at court; Tataka vadha)

objectives.title: Battle Tataka and free her from her curse
objectives.cue: A demoness blocks the path. She was cursed into this form, and only dharma can release her.

completedLine.speaker: Tataka
completedLine.text: You have freed me. I return to the world of spirits, no longer a terror to the innocent.

objectives.citation: Valmiki Ramayana, Bala Kanda, Sarga 25 (Tataka vadha)

objectives.title: Free Ahalya from Gautama’s curse
objectives.cue: A woman turned to stone waits for the touch of Rama. Her curse holds until dharma itself arrives.

completedLine.speaker: Ahalya
completedLine.text: Gautama’s curse is lifted. I am flesh again, and the world is new.

objectives.title: Witness the gathering of princes at Sita’s swayamvara
objectives.cue: Kings and warriors from every corner of Bharata have assembled. Only one can lift Shiva’s bow.

completedLine.speaker: Janaka
completedLine.text: Princes of the earth, the bow awaits. He who bends it shall win my daughter.

objectives.title: Witness the marriages of the four brothers
objectives.cue: Four brides for four brothers. The bonds of dharma extend beyond one couple.

completedLine.speaker: Dasharatha
completedLine.text: My four sons are wed. The kingdom is blessed with four pillars of dharma.

objectives.citation: Valmiki Ramayana (specific sarga not cited — verify in standard recension)

objectives.title: Receive Kausalya’s blessing before leaving with Vishwamitra
objectives.cue: A mother’s blessing is the first armor a warrior wears. Kausalya waits with tears and strength both.

completedLine.speaker: Kausalya
completedLine.text: Go with the gods, my son. A mother’s heart breaks and mends in the same breath when dharma calls.

objectives.title: Witness the story of how Kaikeyi earned her two boons
objectives.cue: In the war with the asuras, Kaikeyi saved Dasharatha’s life. Two boons were promised — unspent until now.

completedLine.speaker: Kaikeyi
completedLine.text: I saved his life with my own hands. He promised me anything. Now I claim what was promised.

dialogue.speaker: Dasharatha
dialogue.text: The gods have heard my prayer. Four sons are born to uphold dharma in this age.

dialogue.speaker: Kausalya
dialogue.text: Rama is not merely my son. He is the hope of all who suffer under adharma.

dialogue.speaker: Vishwamitra
dialogue.text: I am Vishwamitra, once a king, now a sage. I have forged my spirit through ten thousand years of tapas. Now I forge yours.

dialogue.speaker: Tataka
dialogue.text: Who dares enter my forest? I was a woman once, but cursed to become this form. Kill me, and you free me both.

dialogue.speaker: Ahalya
dialogue.text: I have been stone for an age, punished for a moment’s folly. Gautama’s curse said only Rama could restore me.

dialogue.speaker: Janaka
dialogue.text: This bow of Shiva has rested here since the gods walked the earth. Only the worthy may lift it.

dialogue.speaker: Sita
dialogue.text: I have waited for you before I knew your name. The earth herself bore me for this union.

dialogue.speaker: Rama
dialogue.text: I accept Sita not but itself made flesh. Our paths are one.

dialogue.speaker: Shatrughna
dialogue.text: I am the youngest brother, but my devotion is not youngest. Where Bharata goes, I go.

dialogue.speaker: Kausalya
dialogue.text: My son is born. The kingdom shall know him light of Ayodhya.

reward.lore: Balakanda and the descent of Vishnu

acts.title: Ayodhyakanda: The Exile
acts.setup: Dasharatha prepares to crown Rama, but Kaikeyi, influenced by her maid Manthara, remembers two old boons. She demands Bharata be crowned and Rama sent to the forest for fourteen years. Rama accepts exile willingly. Dasharatha dies of grief. Bharata refuses the throne and places Rama’s sandals upon it symbol.
acts.lesson: Dharma must be upheld even at great personal cost. A son’s duty to his father transcends personal ambition.

objectives.title: Accept exile to preserve Dasharatha’s word
objectives.cue: Stand before Kaikeyi and choose duty over anger.

completedLine.speaker: Rama
completedLine.text: Mother, the forest will receive me before Ayodhya sees a broken promise.

objectives.title: Speak with Sita before leaving the palace
objectives.cue: Share the decision with Sita and hear her resolve.

completedLine.speaker: Sita
completedLine.text: I was not made only for palace lamps. I will walk beside you under forest stars.

objectives.title: Witness Dasharatha’s death from a broken heart
objectives.cue: The king who loved his son more than his kingdom cannot survive the separation.

completedLine.speaker: Dasharatha
completedLine.text: Rama... my eyes cannot find you... the light of my house departs... and I am left in darkness.

objectives.title: Witness Bharata refuse the throne and keep Rama’s sandals
objectives.cue: Witness Bharata’s loyalty and the symbol of righteous rule.

completedLine.speaker: Bharata
completedLine.text: I will not sit where my brother belongs. These sandals shall rule Ayodhya until Rama returns.

objectives.title: Cross the Ganga river with Guha the boatman king
objectives.cue: The Ganga separates civilization from wilderness. Guha waits with his boat.

completedLine.speaker: Guha
completedLine.text: I am a king of boatmen, but you treat me brother. The river bows to dharma, not to kings.

objectives.title: Leave Ayodhya for the forest path
objectives.cue: Walk through the southern gate and begin the exile.

completedLine.speaker: Lakshmana
completedLine.text: The city falls behind us, but the path ahead is guarded by purpose.

objectives.citation: Valmiki Ramayana (specific sarga not cited — verify in standard recension)

objectives.title: Witness the citizens of Ayodhya follow Rama into exile
objectives.cue: The people of Ayodhya refuse to let their prince walk alone. They follow him to the forest edge.

completedLine.speaker: Citizen
completedLine.text: We will not return. Where dharma walks, the people of Ayodhya walk.

objectives.title: Witness Bharata rule from Nandigram in Rama’s name
objectives.cue: Bharata refuses the palace. He lives in a hut, ruling’s proxy, the sandals on the throne.

completedLine.speaker: Bharata
completedLine.text: I am not king. I am a servant of the sandals that rule Ayodhya.

objectives.title: Hear Sumitra’s wisdom about serving Rama
objectives.cue: Sumitra does not weep for Lakshmana’s departure. She sees service to Rama highest dharma.

completedLine.speaker: Sumitra
completedLine.text: Rama is your life, Lakshmana. Serve him serve Kausalya — without expectation, without end.

objectives.title: Witness Urmila choose to sleep for fourteen years
objectives.cue: Urmila could have followed Lakshmana. Instead, she chooses to sleep so he may serve without longing.

completedLine.speaker: Urmila
completedLine.text: I will sleep, and in my dreams I will walk beside you. When you return, I shall wake no time has passed.

objectives.title: Witness Manthara poison Kaikeyi’s mind with ambition
objectives.cue: The hunchbacked maid speaks honeyed poison. She reminds Kaikeyi of her power, her beauty, her forgotten worth.

completedLine.speaker: Manthara
completedLine.text: You saved the king’s life. Does he honor you now? No — he crowns Kausalya’s son. Claim what is yours.

dialogue.speaker: Kaikeyi
dialogue.text: The boons your father granted me long ago must be honored today. Let Bharata be crowned and you dwell in the forest for fourteen years.

dialogue.speaker: Rama
dialogue.text: Mother, I go gladly. The forest will receive me before Ayodhya sees a broken promise. A king’s word is the tapas of his people.

dialogue.speaker: Dasharatha
dialogue.text: Rama, my son — the light of my house departs, and I am left in darkness.

dialogue.speaker: Dasharatha
dialogue.text: The palace feels like a tomb. I who ruled three worlds am powerless against the grief of losing one son.

dialogue.speaker: Sita
dialogue.text: I was not made only for palace lamps. I will walk beside you under forest stars. Where you walk, I walk. The forest is not exile if dharma is with us.

dialogue.speaker: Lakshmana
dialogue.text: Then I will guard this path with you. What use is a brother who does not follow into exile?

dialogue.speaker: Manthara
dialogue.text: Kaikeyi, remember your boons. The throne belongs to Bharata. Let Rama rot in the forest while your son rules.

dialogue.speaker: Bharata
dialogue.text: Brother, I kept your sandals on the throne. Ayodhya never accepted another king while you lived. I am merely the keeper of your seat.

dialogue.speaker: Guha
dialogue.text: I am Guha, king of the boatmen. I have prepared my finest boat for you, Rama. The river bows to dharma, not to crowns.

reward.lore: Ayodhyakanda and the meaning of royal duty

acts.title: Aranyakanda: Forest Life
acts.setup: In exile at Panchavati near Nashik, Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana live in peace. They meet Shabari, the ascetic woman who feeds Rama tasted berries. The demoness Shurpanakha tries to seduce Rama and Lakshmana, loses her nose and ears, and incites her brothers Khara and Dushana to attack with fourteen thousand demons. All are killed. Shurpanakha goes to Lanka and tells Ravana about Sita. Ravana, with Maricha transformed golden deer, distracts Rama. Lakshmana is drawn away. Ravana arrives hermit, crosses the Lakshmana Rekha, and abducts Sita. Jatayu tries to rescue her but is mortally wounded.
acts.lesson: Attachment and desire create openings for deception. Ravana exploits Rama’s protective instinct toward Sita, using illusion to separate them.

objectives.title: Meet Shabari and taste her berries
objectives.cue: The ascetic woman has waited her whole life to serve Rama.

completedLine.speaker: Shabari
completedLine.text: I tasted each berry to give you only the sweet ones. My guru said Rama would come to my hut. Today I am complete.

objectives.title: Track the golden deer through the forest
objectives.cue: Follow the glowing trail before the illusion slips away.

completedLine.speaker: Rama
completedLine.text: The deer shines with wonder, but wonder can also be a mask. Maricha is no stranger to deception.

objectives.title: Fire arrows at Maricha disguised among deer illusions
objectives.cue: Three deer appear. Click the real one to fire an arrow.

completedLine.speaker: Rama
completedLine.text: The illusion falls. Maricha’s last breath calls my name, and Lakshmana is drawn away from Sita.

objectives.title: Perform Jatayu’s last rites after he falls defending Sita
objectives.cue: The vulture king gave his life. Honor him with proper rites.

completedLine.speaker: Rama
completedLine.text: You gave your wings for my wife, old friend. I shall name a kingdom after your sacrifice.

objectives.title: Perform Jatayu’s last rites and free his spirit
objectives.cue: The vulture king gave his life for Sita. Honor him with proper funeral rites.

completedLine.speaker: Rama
completedLine.text: You gave your wings for my wife, old friend. I name this kingdom after your sacrifice. Your spirit is free.

objectives.title: Meet Kabandha and help him find redemption
objectives.cue: The cursed demon waits in the forest. Rama’s touch can free him.

completedLine.speaker: Kabandha
completedLine.text: You have buried my cursed body. Now I am free to tell you — go to Sugriva at Rishyamuka. He will help you find Sita.

objectives.title: Witness Sita’s abduction by Ravana
objectives.cue: The moment adharma struck. Remember it.

completedLine.speaker: Ravana
completedLine.text: I take what weakness leaves unguarded. The forest is no place for a queen.

objectives.title: Confront Shurpanakha when she tries to seduce Rama
objectives.cue: Ravana’s sister approaches with desire in her eyes. Her pride will cost her dearly.

completedLine.speaker: Lakshmana
completedLine.text: You came with ill intent. Your nose and ears are the price of your impudence.

objectives.title: Defeat Khara and Dushana’s army of fourteen thousand demons
objectives.cue: The forest trembles. Fourteen thousand demons march under Khara and Dushana. Stand firm.

completedLine.speaker: Rama
completedLine.text: Not one arrow was wasted. Dharma does not count enemies; it counts only rightness.

objectives.title: Witness Lakshmana draw the protective line around Sita
objectives.cue: Lakshmana traces a line of power around the hut. Beyond it, no evil may cross.

completedLine.speaker: Lakshmana
completedLine.text: Sister, do not cross this line. As long stay within, no power on earth can harm you.

objectives.title: Stay at Chitrakuta before moving deeper into the forest
objectives.cue: The mountain of Chitrakuta offers shelter. Here Bharata came to beg Rama back.

completedLine.speaker: Rama
completedLine.text: This mountain remembers Bharata’s tears. We shall rest here before the deeper forest.

objectives.citation: Valmiki Ramayana, Aranya Kanda, Sarga 56 (later: Aranya to Kishkindha transition)

objectives.title: Visit Atri and Anasuya’s ashram and receive their blessings
objectives.cue: The sage Atri and his wife Anasuya dwell here. She has divine garments and wisdom for Sita.

completedLine.speaker: Anasuya
completedLine.text: I give you these garments, Sita. They will never soil, never tear. A wife’s dharma is her true ornament.

objectives.title: Meet Sugriva for the first time at Rishyamuka
objectives.cue: A wounded king of monkeys hides in these hills. His name is Sugriva, and his brother has stolen everything.

completedLine.speaker: Sugriva
completedLine.text: Vali took my throne and my wife. I have only Hanuman and these rocks. Will you help me?

objectives.title: Witness Jambavan remind Hanuman of his divine powers
objectives.cue: Hanuman has forgotten his own strength. Jambavan, oldest of the vanaras, remembers what the wind-god’s son can do.

completedLine.speaker: Jambavan
completedLine.text: You are the son of Vayu. You leaped child to swallow the sun. Remember who you are, Hanuman.

objectives.title: Witness Trijata’s prophetic dream of Rama’s victory
objectives.cue: In Lanka, a demoness named Trijata dreams of Rama’s triumph. She tells Sita what she saw.

completedLine.speaker: Trijata
completedLine.text: I saw Rama on a white chariot, Ravana fallen, and you returning home. The dream was truth, not illusion.

objectives.citation: Valmiki Ramayana, Sundara Kanda, Sargas 24-25 (the dream that gives Sita hope)

objectives.title: Witness Sarama bring comfort to Sita in Ashok Vatika
objectives.cue: Vibhishana’s wife Sarama risks Ravana’s wrath to bring Sita news of Rama’s approach.

completedLine.speaker: Sarama
completedLine.text: My husband serves dharma, not Ravana. Take heart, queen. Rama has crossed the ocean. He is coming.

objectives.citation: Valmiki Ramayana, Sundara Kanda, Sarga 23 (Sarama the vulture-kind mother of Jatayu)

dialogue.speaker: Shabari
dialogue.text: I am old and low-born, but my guru said Rama would come to my hut. I have tasted each berry to give you only the sweet ones.

dialogue.speaker: Rama
dialogue.text: Your devotion is greater than any birth. The forest has no caste, and dharma accepts all who serve it.

dialogue.speaker: Anasuya
dialogue.text: I am Anasuya, wife of Atri. Sita, take this celestial garment. It will never soil, for purity is its own protection.

dialogue.speaker: Kabandha
dialogue.text: I was cursed to become this headless form. Only one who knows his true self can free me. Bury my body, and I shall reveal what I know.

dialogue.speaker: Sita
dialogue.text: That deer shines with wonder, but wonder can also be a mask. Do not follow it too far, Rama. I fear what lies behind such beauty.

dialogue.speaker: Lakshmana
dialogue.text: Brother, I hear your call in the distance. But I swore not to leave Sita. The line I drew around our hut — let it protect her.

dialogue.speaker: Ravana
dialogue.text: Alms, noble lady. A hermit seeks only what the forest gives freely. Step beyond that line, and I shall reveal what this forest truly holds.

dialogue.speaker: Jatayu
dialogue.text: Ravana! You shall not take her while I yet breathe. I am old, but my wings still remember the sky.

dialogue.speaker: Rama
dialogue.text: You gave your wings for my wife, old friend. I shall name a kingdom after your sacrifice. Your spirit is free now.

dialogue.speaker: Jambavan
dialogue.text: I am older than the mountains. I have seen ages rise and fall. But I have never seen a devotion like yours, Rama.

dialogue.speaker: Trijata
dialogue.text: I am a demoness, but my dreams are not. I saw Rama victorious, and I saw you returning to Ayodhya. Believe me.

reward.lore: Aranyakanda and the cost of illusion

acts.title: Kishkindhakanda: Alliance
acts.setup: Rama and Lakshmana reach Kishkindha and form an alliance with the exiled monkey king Sugriva. Rama kills Vali and restores Sugriva’s throne. In return, Sugriva sends his vanara army to search for Sita. Hanuman, son of the wind, leaps across the ocean to Lanka.
acts.lesson: True devotion knows no boundaries. Hanuman’s leap demonstrates that faith and service can overcome impossible obstacles.

objectives.title: Confront Vali and restore Sugriva’s throne
objectives.cue: Vali is cursed — half his strength goes to his opponent in combat. Choose how to approach.

completedLine.speaker: Rama
completedLine.text: I struck from hiding, hunter strikes a deer. Vali, you were my friend’s enemy, and I chose dharma over glory.

objectives.title: Witness Tara’s mourning for Vali
objectives.cue: Tara mourns her husband. Witness her grief.

completedLine.speaker: Tara
completedLine.text: My husband was mighty, but his pride was mightier. I warned him. Now I mourn him.

objectives.title: Form alliance with Sugriva and the vanaras
objectives.cue: Sugriva promises his army in exchange for his throne.

completedLine.speaker: Sugriva
completedLine.text: Rama, I too have lost my kingdom to my brother. Let us help each other.

objectives.title: Witness Sugriva’s coronation of Kishkindha
objectives.cue: The exile becomes king. Dharma restores what was stolen.

completedLine.speaker: Sugriva
completedLine.text: Rama, I am king because of your arrow. My throne is yours. My army is yours. My life is yours.

objectives.title: Send the vanara search parties in four directions
objectives.cue: The search begins. Sita will be found.

completedLine.speaker: Hanuman
completedLine.text: I am small before Rama, but his name makes the impossible crossable. I shall leap across the ocean and find Sita.

objectives.citation: Valmiki Ramayana, Kishkindha Kanda, Sargas 41-62 (the monkey search begins)

objectives.title: Endure the four-month monsoon delay before the search
objectives.cue: The monsoon arrives. The vanaras cannot cross rivers in flood. Patience becomes the only weapon.

completedLine.speaker: Rama
completedLine.text: Even the rain serves dharma. When the waters recede, the search will resume with greater strength.

objectives.title: Witness Lakshmana’s anger at Sugriva’s forgetfulness
objectives.cue: Sugriva has forgotten his promise, lost in wine and women. Lakshmana storms the gates.

completedLine.speaker: Lakshmana
completedLine.text: We gave you a kingdom and you repay us with forgetfulness? My brother’s sorrow is not a season to be waited out.

objectives.title: Send Angada on a diplomatic mission to Ravana
objectives.cue: Before war, a final word of peace. Angada carries the message of dharma to Ravana’s court.

completedLine.speaker: Angada
completedLine.text: Ravana laughed at our offer of peace. He has chosen war. We have chosen dharma.

objectives.title: Hear Sampati reveal Sita’s location in Lanka
objectives.cue: Jatayu’s brother Sampati, old and wingless, saw Ravana carry Sita southward. His memory is the map.

completedLine.speaker: Sampati
completedLine.text: I saw the demon king fly south with a woman in his chariot. She wept Rama’s name across the sky.

objectives.title: Witness Hanuman doubt his ability to cross the ocean
objectives.cue: Even the son of the wind hesitates before the infinite ocean. Jambavan must remind him of his birthright.

completedLine.speaker: Hanuman
completedLine.text: The ocean is vast, and I am small. How can I cross what the gods themselves fear?

dialogue.speaker: Sugriva
dialogue.text: Rama, I too have lost my kingdom to my brother Vali. Let us help each other — you help me reclaim Kishkindha, and my vanaras shall help you find Sita.

dialogue.speaker: Vali
dialogue.text: You challenge me, stranger? I have defeated demons and gods alike. No one who faces me survives.

dialogue.speaker: Tara
dialogue.text: My husband was mighty, but his pride was mightier. I warned him not to trust the stranger in the forest.

dialogue.speaker: Jambavan
dialogue.text: Hanuman, son of the wind, your time has come. Leap across the ocean and find the queen of Ayodhya.

dialogue.speaker: Sampati
dialogue.text: I am Jatayu’s brother. I saw Ravana carry Sita southward, toward Lanka. That is where you must search.

reward.lore: Kishkindhakanda and the bond of exile

acts.title: Sundarakanda: Ocean Leaper
acts.setup: Hanuman, son of the wind, leaps across the ocean to Lanka. He meets Mount Mainaka, which rises from the sea to offer rest. He defeats the sea serpent Surasa. He finds Sita in Ashok Vatika, delivers Rama’s ring, and receives her chudamani. When captured, his tail is set on fire, and he burns Lanka before escaping.
acts.lesson: Devotion makes the impossible possible. Hanuman’s leap is not just physical — it is the leap of faith across any divide.

objectives.title: Leap across the ocean to Lanka
objectives.cue: Take the leap that no other being dares.

completedLine.speaker: Hanuman
completedLine.text: The ocean is vast, but Rama’s name makes me light feather. I see Lanka on the horizon.

objectives.title: Burn Lanka with your flaming tail
objectives.cue: The demons set your tail on fire. Turn their weapon against them.

completedLine.speaker: Hanuman
completedLine.text: You lit my tail to humiliate me. Now watch your city burn. This is what happens when you touch what belongs to Rama.

objectives.title: Pass Surasa’s test by showing your true form
objectives.cue: The sea serpent opens her jaws. Expand to match her size.

completedLine.speaker: Hanuman
completedLine.text: I grew to fill your mouth, then shrank to escape. Size is illusion. Purpose is real.

objectives.title: Give Rama’s ring to Sita and receive her chudamani
objectives.cue: The ring is proof. The chudamani is hope.

completedLine.speaker: Sita
completedLine.text: This ring carries Rama’s promise. Take my chudamani and tell him I wait, unbroken, in Ashok Vatika.

objectives.title: Escape Ravana’s court after your tail is set on fire
objectives.cue: They bound you and lit your tail. Now burn their city and leap free.

completedLine.speaker: Hanuman
completedLine.text: You lit my tail to shame me. Now watch Lanka burn. This is the price of touching what belongs to Rama.

objectives.title: Explore Lanka under cover of darkness to find Sita
objectives.cue: The city sleeps, but its demons do not. Move through the shadows and find the queen.

completedLine.speaker: Hanuman
completedLine.text: I have seen Lanka’s walls, its gardens, and its throne. Now I must find where Sita waits.

objectives.title: Defeat Simhika, the shadow demoness who swallows the wind
objectives.cue: A shadow opens its jaws in mid-air. Simhika reaches for your shadow before you can escape.

completedLine.speaker: Hanuman
completedLine.text: You tried to swallow my shadow, but my shadow is’s name.

objectives.title: Destroy the Ashok Vatika grove after giving Sita hope
objectives.cue: The demons attack after you deliver Rama’s ring. Let the grove feel your wrath.

completedLine.speaker: Hanuman
completedLine.text: I came messenger, but adharma turned me into a storm. Let Lanka remember this day.

objectives.citation: Valmiki Ramayana, Sundara Kanda, Sargas 22-23 (and 30-35 for the meeting)

objectives.title: Return to Rama and bring word that Sita has been found
objectives.cue: The ocean must be crossed again, but this time carrying hope instead of doubt.

completedLine.speaker: Rama
completedLine.text: You have seen her? She lives? Then the sun has risen again in my heart.

objectives.citation: Valmiki Ramayana, Yuddha Kanda, Sargas 121-128 (the return to Ayodhya)

objectives.title: Witness Hanuman captured by Indrajit’s Brahmastra
objectives.cue: Indrajit’s divine weapon binds Hanuman. Even the son of the wind cannot escape the laws of the creator.

completedLine.speaker: Indrajit
completedLine.text: You thought fire could not be bound? The Brahmastra answers to Brahma alone.

objectives.citation: Valmiki Ramayana, Yuddha Kanda, Sarga 107 (the release of the brahmastra)

objectives.title: Witness Vibhishana’s house spared during the burning of Lanka
objectives.cue: The city burns, but one house stands untouched. Hanuman knows the difference between enemy and ally.

completedLine.speaker: Vibhishana
completedLine.text: You burned Lanka but spared my home. This is the discernment of dharma — to destroy adharma, not the righteous.

objectives.citation: Valmiki Ramayana, Yuddha Kanda, Sargas 17-20 (and throughout the war)

objectives.title: Witness Hanuman shrink to the size of a cat to enter Lanka
objectives.cue: The son of the wind can be smaller than a spark. He shrinks to slip past Lanka’s gates unseen.

completedLine.speaker: Hanuman
completedLine.text: I am smaller than a cat, lighter than a thought. Lanka’s walls are mountains to others, but a crack to me.

objectives.title: Witness Hanuman grow to the size of a mountain to defeat guards
objectives.cue: When the demons attack, Hanuman remembers his true size. He becomes a mountain with teeth.

completedLine.speaker: Hanuman
completedLine.text: You wanted to see my true form? Here it is. I am the son of Vayu, and your city is my playground.

objectives.title: Witness Sita give her chudamani to Hanuman
objectives.cue: Sita has kept Rama’s ring. Now she gives her own jewel — the chudamani that marked her’s daughter.

completedLine.speaker: Sita
completedLine.text: Take this chudamani. It has adorned my head since my wedding day. Let it tell Rama I have kept my vows.

dialogue.speaker: Mainaka
dialogue.text: Son of the wind, rest upon me. The ocean is long and you have far to go.

dialogue.speaker: Surasa
dialogue.text: The gods have sent me to test you. Only one who knows his true size can pass.

dialogue.speaker: Simhika
dialogue.text: I am the shadow that swallows the wind itself. No one escapes my darkness.

dialogue.speaker: Hanuman
dialogue.text: I am small before Rama, but his name makes the impossible crossable. I shall write his name upon every stone.

dialogue.speaker: Sita
dialogue.text: This ring carries Rama’s promise. Hope has reached Lanka before the army. Tell him I wait, unbroken, in Ashok Vatika.

dialogue.speaker: Ravana
dialogue.text: A monkey in my city? Guards, bind him. We shall teach him the price of trespass.

dialogue.speaker: Trijata
dialogue.text: Do not fear, Sita. I watch over you. Even in Ravana’s garden, kindness finds a way.

dialogue.speaker: Sarama
dialogue.text: Sita, my husband Vibhishana speaks for your return. Take heart. Rama is coming.

acts.title: Yuddhakanda: Bridge and War
acts.setup: Rama’s army reaches the southern ocean. Nala and Nila, sons of the divine architect Vishvakarma, direct the vanaras to build a bridge of stones across the sea. Each stone is inscribed with Rama’s name. The army crosses into Lanka. The great battle lasts ten days. Kumbhakarna is awakened and killed. Indrajit uses maya warfare but is defeated. Lakshmana is struck down and revived by Sanjeevani. Rama duels Ravana and defeats him. Sita undergoes Agni Pariksha — the trial by fire — and emerges unharmed.
acts.lesson: Justice requires both patience and strength. Rama offers Ravana every chance to return Sita peacefully before resorting to war.

objectives.title: Collect bridge stones for the vanara builders
objectives.cue: Gather eight stones so the bridge can begin.

completedLine.speaker: Rama
completedLine.text: One stone is small. Many small vows become a road across the sea.

objectives.citation: Valmiki Ramayana, Yuddha Kanda, Sargas 21-23 (the bridge of stones)

objectives.title: Place stones into the ocean path
objectives.cue: Place the gathered stones and watch the path take shape.

completedLine.speaker: Nala
completedLine.text: The bridge rises because every hand remembers why it works.

objectives.citation: Valmiki Ramayana, Yuddha Kanda, Sargas 21-23 (the bridge of stones)

objectives.title: Cross Rama Setu toward Lanka
objectives.cue: Cross the completed bridge and carry the vow into battle.

completedLine.speaker: Rama
completedLine.text: The ocean has become a path. Now justice must become action.

objectives.citation: Valmiki Ramayana, Yuddha Kanda, Sarga 23 (the crossing)

objectives.title: Defeat Kumbhakarna, the giant brother of Ravana
objectives.cue: The giant has been awakened. He devours armies whole.

completedLine.speaker: Rama
completedLine.text: Even a mountain must fall when dharma stands against it.

objectives.citation: Valmiki Ramayana, Yuddha Kanda, Sargas 56-60 (Kumbhakarna's last battle)

objectives.title: Defeat Indrajit, master of illusion warfare
objectives.cue: He uses maya to strike from shadows. Find the real one.

completedLine.speaker: Lakshmana
completedLine.text: The illusion falls. Now we face the real enemy.

objectives.citation: Valmiki Ramayana, Yuddha Kanda, Sargas 78-83 (the duel and fall of Indrajit)

objectives.title: Duel Ravana in the final battle
objectives.cue: The ten-headed king stands before you. Choose your moment.

completedLine.speaker: Rama
completedLine.text: I gave you every chance to return Sita. Now your pride meets its end.

objectives.title: Witness Lakshmana fall to Indrajit’s Nagapasha serpent arrows
objectives.cue: The serpent arrows strike. Lakshmana collapses.

completedLine.speaker: Rama
completedLine.text: My brother lies still. The serpent venom courses through him. Hanuman, fly. Bring the mountain of healing.

objectives.title: Fetch the Sanjeevani mountain to revive Lakshmana
objectives.cue: The mountain of healing grows in the Himalayas. Carry it whole.

completedLine.speaker: Sushena
completedLine.text: Hanuman has brought the entire mountain. From its herbs, we brew the cure. Lakshmana shall rise again.

objectives.citation: Valmiki Ramayana (specific sarga not cited — verify in standard recension)

objectives.title: Witness Vibhishana crowned king of Lanka
objectives.cue: Adharma has fallen. Dharma must now rule Lanka.

completedLine.speaker: Vibhishana
completedLine.text: I did not seek this throne. But dharma must have a seat in Lanka. I shall rule by righteousness.

objectives.citation: Valmiki Ramayana, Yuddha Kanda, Sargas 17-20 (and throughout the war)

objectives.title: Witness Sita’s trial by fire
objectives.cue: The fire must testify. Witness the truth.

completedLine.speaker: Sita
completedLine.text: I call upon Agni himself. If I am pure, let him protect me. If not, let me burn.

objectives.title: Ask the ocean for passage to Lanka
objectives.cue: The sea stands between you and justice. Ask it to yield, or force it to part.

completedLine.speaker: Varuna
completedLine.text: I yield, Rama. Nala and Nila shall build your bridge. My waters will hold the stones that bear your name.

objectives.title: Lakshmana defeats Indrajit using the Aindra weapon
objectives.cue: The son of Ravana hides in illusion. Only one who has conquered sleep can strike the true form.

completedLine.speaker: Lakshmana
completedLine.text: Your maya falls before the Aindra weapon. The shadows cannot hide you from dharma.

objectives.title: Inscribe Rama’s name on the bridge stones
objectives.cue: Nala and Nila place the stones, but only Rama’s name makes them float. Write upon each one.

completedLine.speaker: Nala
completedLine.text: The stones bear your name and they float. The ocean itself bows to the power of faith.

objectives.citation: Valmiki Ramayana, Yuddha Kanda, Sargas 21-23 (the bridge of stones)

objectives.title: Capture and release Ravana’s spies Shuka and Sarana
objectives.cue: Ravana’s spies have been caught in the camp. Choose their fate — mercy or punishment.

completedLine.speaker: Shuka
completedLine.text: You feed us and send us back to our master with truth. This is the kingship of Rama.

objectives.citation: Valmiki Ramayana, Yuddha Kanda, Sargas 45-49 (Indrajit's nagapasha)

objectives.title: Witness Kumbhakarna’s tragic loyalty before battle
objectives.cue: Kumbhakarna knows Ravana is wrong, but blood binds him. His sleep has been his only escape from sorrow.

completedLine.speaker: Kumbhakarna
completedLine.text: I told him to return Sita. He did not listen. Now I must fight for a brother I cannot save from himself.

objectives.title: Witness Mandodari’s final plea to Ravana
objectives.cue: Ravana’s wife Mandodari has warned him a hundred times. This is her last attempt before the city burns.

completedLine.speaker: Mandodari
completedLine.text: Husband, I have been your wife for ten thousand years. I beg you — return her. Do not make me a widow.

objectives.citation: Valmiki Ramayana, Yuddha Kanda, Sarga 103 (Mandodari's final plea)

objectives.title: Witness Vibhishana formally defect to Rama’s side
objectives.cue: Vibhishana has warned Ravana ten times. Now he kneels before Rama, offering Lanka’s secrets and his life.

completedLine.speaker: Vibhishana
completedLine.text: I am no traitor. I am a man who chose dharma over blood. Use my knowledge, Rama. Save Sita.

objectives.citation: Valmiki Ramayana, Yuddha Kanda, Sargas 17-20 (and throughout the war)

dialogue.speaker: Rama
dialogue.text: Each stone carries a name, each name carries faith, and faith becomes the bridge. The ocean itself shall yield to dharma.

dialogue.speaker: Hanuman
dialogue.text: I shall write Rama’s name upon every stone. No force in the three worlds can break such a bridge.

dialogue.speaker: Nala
dialogue.text: I am the son of Vishvakarma, the divine architect. Under my direction, these stones shall hold firm.

dialogue.speaker: Kumbhakarna
dialogue.text: Brother, you should have listened to Vibhishana. But I am bound by blood. Let me sleep again after this battle.

dialogue.speaker: Indrajit
dialogue.text: I have defeated Indra himself. What chance do mortals have against my maya?

dialogue.speaker: Vibhishana
dialogue.text: Brother, return Sita. Dharma is not defeated by strength of arms. Even the gods tremble before adharma.

dialogue.speaker: Ravana
dialogue.text: You come to my city with an army of monkeys and expect to leave with what I have taken? I am Ravana — scholar of the Vedas, devotee of Shiva, king of the three worlds.

dialogue.speaker: Mandodari
dialogue.text: Husband, the omens are against us. Return the queen of Ayodhya before our city is ash.

dialogue.speaker: Nila
dialogue.text: I am Nala, son of Vishvakarma. With Nala beside me, these stones will hold itself.

dialogue.speaker: Kumbhakarna
dialogue.text: I told my brother to return Sita. He would not listen. Now I must fight for a king I cannot save.

reward.lore: Rama Setu and the vanara army

acts.title: Uttarakanda: Return and Rule
acts.setup: After Ravana’s defeat, Rama, Sita, Lakshmana, and Hanuman return to Ayodhya in the Pushpaka Vimana. Bharata joyfully returns the kingdom. But Rama’s subjects doubt Sita’s purity. Though she proved herself through Agni Pariksha, Rama banishes pregnant Sita to the forest to satisfy public opinion. She lives with Valmiki, bears twins Lava and Kusha, who later defeat Rama’s army unintentionally.
acts.lesson: Victory is not conquest alone; it is the restoration of justice. Rama Rajya represents the ideal where the king serves dharma above all.

objectives.title: Witness the fall of Ravana and the end of adharma
objectives.cue: Stand before the battle memory and witness adharma fall.

completedLine.speaker: Rama
completedLine.text: Power without righteousness collapses beneath its own weight.

objectives.citation: Valmiki Ramayana, Yuddha Kanda, Sargas 108-115 (after Ravana's fall)

objectives.title: Return to Ayodhya in the Pushpaka Vimana
objectives.cue: Return to Ayodhya, where duty must become public care.

completedLine.speaker: Sita
completedLine.text: The road returns us home, but exile has taught us how to see the kingdom.

objectives.citation: Valmiki Ramayana, Yuddha Kanda, Sargas 121-128 (the return to Ayodhya)

objectives.title: Light the lamps of Ayodhya
objectives.cue: Light fourteen lamps for the years of exile and the return of justice.

completedLine.speaker: Hanuman
completedLine.text: Where these lamps are lit, the story will continue to serve.

objectives.citation: Valmiki Ramayana, Yuddha Kanda, Sarga 121 (the city lit with lamps)

objectives.title: The painful decision regarding Sita
objectives.cue: The people doubt. Dharma demands the king be above reproach. Choose.

completedLine.speaker: Rama
completedLine.text: I know your purity, Sita. But the king must be above suspicion. The forest will shelter you where the palace cannot.

objectives.title: Capture the Ashvamedha horse sent by Rama
objectives.cue: The horse of the great sacrifice roams free. The twins guard it.

completedLine.speaker: Lava
completedLine.text: This horse is ours now. Whoever seeks it must first answer to our swords.

objectives.citation: Valmiki Ramayana, Uttara Kanda, Sargas 14-19 (the horse sacrifice)

objectives.title: Meet Lava and Kusha in Valmiki’s ashram
objectives.cue: Your sons have grown in the forest. Hear them sing your story.

completedLine.speaker: Valmiki
completedLine.text: These boys know the Ramayana song. They have defeated your army without knowing whose sons they are.

objectives.citation: Valmiki Ramayana, Uttara Kanda, Sargas 12-13, 27-35 (Lava and Kusha)

objectives.title: Witness Rama’s coronation of Ayodhya
objectives.cue: The ideal king takes the throne. Rama Rajya begins.

completedLine.speaker: Vasishtha
completedLine.text: Rama is crowned. Not by power, but by dharma. Let all kingdoms learn from this day.

objectives.title: Witness the birth of Lava and Kusha in Valmiki’s ashram
objectives.cue: In the forest where Sita was sent, two sons are born. The Ramayana begins again in their cries.

completedLine.speaker: Valmiki
completedLine.text: These children are the sons of dharma itself. I shall teach them the story their father lived.

objectives.title: Witness Rama and Sita’s reunion in the forest
objectives.cue: After years of separation, the king meets his queen again where the forest meets the river.

completedLine.speaker: Sita
completedLine.text: I have waited, not in sorrow but in silence. The earth knows my truth, and that is enough.

objectives.title: Witness Lava and Kusha defeat Rama’s army
objectives.cue: The boys trained in Valmiki’s ashram have grown into warriors. They stand against Ayodhya’s army.

completedLine.speaker: Lava
completedLine.text: We do not know whose sons we are. But we know the Ramayana, and that is enough to defeat any army.

objectives.citation: Valmiki Ramayana, Uttara Kanda, Sargas 12-13, 27-35 (Lava and Kusha)

objectives.title: Witness Hanuman recognize Rama’s sons in the battlefield
objectives.cue: Hanuman sees himself in these boys — the same devotion, the same fire. He knows whose blood they carry.

completedLine.speaker: Hanuman
completedLine.text: I see Rama’s eyes in you both. The same steadiness, the same sorrow. You are your father’s sons.

objectives.title: Witness Rama divide the kingdom between Lava and Kusha
objectives.cue: Before departing, the ideal king ensures his legacy. Lava receives the north, Kusha the south.

completedLine.speaker: Rama
completedLine.text: Lava, the north is yours. Kusha, the south is yours. Rule not by power, but by the dharma I tried to live.

objectives.citation: Valmiki Ramayana, Uttara Kanda, Sargas 38-44 (the kingdom divided)

objectives.title: Witness Shatrughna defeat the demon Lavana of Madhupura
objectives.cue: While Rama ruled, Shatrughna guarded the borders. The demon Lavana of Madhupura demanded tribute.

completedLine.speaker: Shatrughna
completedLine.text: You demanded tribute from Rama’s kingdom? I am the fourth brother, and I do not bargain with demons.

objectives.citation: Valmiki Ramayana, Uttara Kanda, Sargas 36-40 (Shatrughna's victory over Lavanasura)

objectives.title: Witness the golden age of Rama Rajya
objectives.cue: For ten thousand years, Rama ruled. No child died young, no crop failed, no lie was spoken in his kingdom.

completedLine.speaker: Citizen
completedLine.text: In Rama Rajya, the rain came when it should, the harvest was always enough, and no door needed a lock.

objectives.title: Witness the citizens whisper doubts about Sita’s purity
objectives.cue: Even in Rama Rajya, doubt finds a way. The people whisper behind closed doors about the queen who lived in Lanka.

completedLine.speaker: Citizen
completedLine.text: She lived in Ravana’s house for months. How can we call her pure, even if the fire said so?

dialogue.speaker: Rama
dialogue.text: A kingdom is not won by power alone. It is held by justice. Let all beings know — Rama Rajya is not rule by force, but by dharma.

dialogue.speaker: Sita
dialogue.text: Fourteen years in the forest taught me more than fourteen years in the palace. The kingdom looks different from the ground than from the throne.

dialogue.speaker: Hanuman
dialogue.text: Where Rama’s story is remembered, service never ends. I shall live name is spoken.

dialogue.speaker: Bharata
dialogue.text: Brother, I kept your sandals on the throne. Ayodhya never accepted another king while you lived.

dialogue.speaker: Vasishtha
dialogue.text: The ideal king has returned. Let the people know that dharma itself now walks among them.

dialogue.speaker: Valmiki
dialogue.text: Sita dwells in my ashram. She has borne you two sons. They sing the Ramayana song, not knowing they are its heroes.

dialogue.speaker: Lava
dialogue.text: We are the sons of the forest. Our mother is the earth, our father is the wind, and our guru is Valmiki.

dialogue.speaker: Urmila
dialogue.text: I have slept for fourteen years so my husband could serve without longing. I wake now, and the world is new.

dialogue.speaker: Mandavi
dialogue.text: Bharata ruled from a hut, wearing bark. I ruled beside him in silence. That is the dharma of a wife.

reward.lore: Uttarakanda and the price of kingship

acts.title: Uttarakanda: Return to Earth
acts.setup: Sita is called to prove her purity once more before the court of Ayodhya. She calls upon her mother, the Earth goddess Prithvi. The earth opens and receives her. Rama rules for many years but eventually follows her, departing from the world. The Ramayana becomes the song of the ages.
acts.lesson: Sita’s return to Earth teaches that the ideal and the real cannot always coexist. Dharma demands sacrifice from those who uphold it most purely.

objectives.title: Witness Sita’s second trial before the court
objectives.cue: Sita must prove herself again. The earth itself will testify.

completedLine.speaker: Sita
completedLine.text: I have proved myself once before Agni. Now I call upon my mother, the Earth. If I am pure, let her receive me.

objectives.title: Witness Sita’s return to Earth
objectives.cue: The earth opens. Sita returns to her mother.

completedLine.speaker: Prithvi
completedLine.text: My daughter, come home. You have suffered enough for this world.

objectives.citation: Valmiki Ramayana, Yuddha Kanda, Sargas 121-128 (the return to Ayodhya)

objectives.title: Witness Rama’s departure from the world
objectives.cue: The king who was more than a king returns to the source.

completedLine.speaker: Rama
completedLine.text: I came to establish dharma. Now I return to where I came from. The story continues in those who remember it.

objectives.title: Witness Valmiki teach Lava and Kusha the Ramayana
objectives.cue: In the forest ashram, Valmiki composes the epic in verse. The twins learn it song, not knowing they are its heroes.

completedLine.speaker: Valmiki
completedLine.text: Sing this song, children. Let the meter carry the meaning. The story must live in the heart, not just the ear.

objectives.citation: Valmiki Ramayana, Uttara Kanda, Sargas 12-13, 27-35 (Lava and Kusha)

objectives.title: Witness Sita bless the earth before returning to her mother
objectives.cue: Before the earth opens, Sita turns to the world one last time. Her blessing becomes the harvest.

completedLine.speaker: Sita
completedLine.text: I have loved this earth, its rivers, its people. May those who remember me find strength in their own purity.

objectives.citation: Valmiki Ramayana, Yuddha Kanda, Sargas 121-128 (the return to Ayodhya)

dialogue.speaker: Sita
dialogue.text: I have proved myself once before Agni. Now I call upon my mother, the Earth. If I am pure, let her receive me. If not, let me be swallowed.

dialogue.speaker: Prithvi
dialogue.text: My daughter, come home. You have suffered enough for this world. I bore you for the sake of dharma, and now I reclaim you.

dialogue.speaker: Rama
dialogue.text: I came to establish dharma. Now I return to where I came from. The story continues in those who remember it. Teach your children.

dialogue.speaker: Valmiki
dialogue.text: Thus ends the Ramayana of Valmiki. Rama is dharma. Sita is the earth. Lakshmana is devotion. Hanuman is service. The story never ends.

reward.lore: The full journey from birth to return

corpus_meta.scope: Curated Ramayana pilot — 98 objectives across 8 acts (7 kāṇḍas + return)
corpus_meta.approach: Each objective is original English paraphrase, not a translation of any particular published edition of the Valmiki Ramayana. Each objective carries a `citation` field pointing to the sarga(s) of the standard recension where the event is located, so a reader can cross-reference. Sanskrit shlokas are not included — those would require a real source text (Bibek Debroy, Griffith, Shastri, or similar). Use this for gameplay/UX; for source-faithful textual study, please use a published translation.
corpus_meta.sarga_numbering_note: Sarga numbers are based on the standard critical recension. Some recensions (e.g. the Vulgate / Southern recension) renumber certain sargas. Treat these as approximate pointers, not absolute line numbers.
corpus_meta.warning: Dialogue text in `completedLine.text` and `dialogue[*].text` is original paraphrase. It conveys the dramatic meaning of the event but is not a literal translation of any Sanskrit verse. The Valmiki Ramayana is the intellectual source for the *sequence of events* and the *characters*, but the words are not from Valmiki's text.
corpus_meta.sarga_count_estimate: Valmiki Ramayana total sargas: ~500 across 7 kāṇḍas
